seo.title: Admin Dashboard — Real-Time Network Control | Hotline HQ
seo.description: Full network visibility from one screen. Real-time user status, broadcast analytics, room management, phone health monitoring, and system diagnostics.
seo.keywords: hotline admin panel, network management dashboard, broadcast analytics, real-time monitoring, conference room management

hero.heading: Full control from one screen
hero.lede: Real-time user status, broadcast analytics, room management, phone health monitoring, and system diagnostics. Everything a network operator needs — nothing they don't.

problem.heading: Running a network blind is dangerous
problem.text: Without visibility, operators fly blind. Are members online? Are rooms active? Are phones even working? Problems slide by for hours before anyone notices. The dashboard puts the full picture in front of you — live, on one screen.

steps.title: Live overview
steps.desc: The dashboard homepage shows real-time stats: users online, in conference, active broadcasts, with a scrolling event ticker and room activity timelines.

steps.title: User management
steps.desc: See every member's status — online, offline, muted, talking, error — with smart suggestions for common issues. Bulk actions: reconnect all, mute all, kick all.

steps.title: Broadcast analytics
steps.desc: Charts for broadcast volume, response rates, top broadcasters, peak hours. Filter by room, time range, answered or unanswered.

steps.title: System health
steps.desc: FreeSWITCH connection status, server uptime, database size, memory usage, SIP registration count. Auto-alerts fire when something looks off.

benefits.title: Real-time SSE updates
benefits.desc: Every dashboard page updates live via Server-Sent Events. No page refreshes, no polling — changes show up the instant they happen.

benefits.title: Smart diagnostics
benefits.desc: The user detail panel flags problems for you: "UDP/NAT issue detected," "Phone unreachable for 5 minutes," "Stuck in connecting state." You know what to fix without digging.

benefits.title: Role-based access
benefits.desc: Three admin roles: Admin (full access), Editor (users and rooms), Analytics (read-only). Each operator sees only what they need.

benefits.title: Remote phone control
benefits.desc: Reboot phones, push configurations, update SIP servers, rebind devices — all from the dashboard. No driving out to the yard.

scenario.heading: Real-world: diagnosing a silent yard
scenario.text: An operator notices a yard that's been online but silent for 3 days. They open the user detail panel and see: "No syslog events in 72 hours — phone may be powered off or network-disconnected." They tap "Reboot" to send a remote restart. 30 seconds later, the phone reconnects and the yard is back on the hotline.

faqs.q: Can multiple admins use the dashboard simultaneously?
faqs.a: Yes. The dashboard handles multiple admin sessions at once. All admins see the same real-time data via SSE.

faqs.q: What analytics are available?
faqs.a: Broadcast volume charts, response rate tracking, hourly distribution, top broadcasters by room, user availability timelines, and room capacity utilization.

faqs.q: Can I export data from the dashboard?
faqs.a: Yes. FreeSWITCH logs, phone logs, and server logs all support CSV export. Broadcast data is also available through the API.

faqs.q: Is there an API for integrating with other systems?
faqs.a: Yes. The admin API covers user management, room management, and broadcast data. Generate and revoke API keys from the Settings page.

seo.title: Always-On Voice Network — 24/7 Live Hotline | Hotline HQ
seo.description: A live voice network that runs 24/7 with zero dialing. Members connect the moment their phone powers on. Built for industries where speed wins the sale.
seo.keywords: always on hotline, live voice network, 24/7 hotline, auto parts hotline, voice conference network

hero.heading: A voice network that never sleeps
hero.lede: No dialing. No hold music. No voicemail. Every member is connected to their regional room the moment their phone powers on — and stays connected all day, every day.

problem.heading: The old way wastes hours
problem.text: Locating inventory the old way means calling yard after yard, sitting on hold, leaving voicemails that never get returned. By the time you track down the part, the customer's already gone. A hotline flips that — broadcast once, and every yard in your region hears it live.

steps.title: Phone powers on
steps.desc: The preconfigured desk phone or web app registers with FreeSWITCH automatically and drops the member into their regional room.

steps.title: Always listening
steps.desc: Members hear every broadcast in their room in real time. No ringing, no answering — audio flows like an open radio channel.

steps.title: Unmute to broadcast
steps.desc: Need a part? Unmute — off-hook on the desk phone, tap on mobile — and speak. Every member in the room hears it instantly.

steps.title: Response in seconds
steps.desc: A yard with the part unmutes to respond. Average response time across the network: 2 seconds. The deal happens live, on air.

benefits.title: Zero friction
benefits.desc: No phone numbers to remember, no contacts to manage, no apps to open. The connection is just there.

benefits.title: Regional rooms
benefits.desc: 20+ rooms organized by geography. Members only hear broadcasts relevant to their area — California, Texas, Florida, and more.

benefits.title: Automatic failover
benefits.desc: If a connection drops, the system retries 5 times at 5-second intervals. Still down? It escalates to 15-minute, 30-minute, and 1-hour fallback attempts.

benefits.title: Works while you work
benefits.desc: The hotline runs in the background. Go about your day — you only tune in when you hear a part you've got.

scenario.heading: Real-world: finding a 2019 Camry bumper
scenario.text: A body shop calls their local yard needing a 2019 Toyota Camry front bumper. The yard doesn't have it, so the rep picks up the hotline phone and says "Looking for a 2019 Camry front bumper, any color." Within 2 seconds, a yard 40 miles away responds: "I've got one, silver, $180." The deal is done before the body shop customer even sits down.

faqs.q: Do members need to dial a number to connect?
faqs.a: No. The desk phone or web app connects on its own when powered on. No number to dial, no conference code to punch in.

faqs.q: What happens if the internet goes down?
faqs.a: The system catches the drop and retries — 5 attempts at 5-second intervals, then longer fallbacks at 15, 30, and 60 minutes. When connectivity comes back, the phone reconnects on its own.

faqs.q: Can members be in multiple rooms at once?
faqs.a: Members belong to one room at a time, but they can switch rooms instantly from the web dashboard or their desk phone softkeys.

faqs.q: Is there a limit to how many members can be in a room?
faqs.a: No hard limit. Our largest rooms run 100+ members connected at once with no audio quality issues.

faqs.q: Does the network run on weekends and holidays?
faqs.a: Yes. 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, 365 days a year. No schedule, no downtime windows.

seo.title: Works On Any Device — Phone, Mobile, Desktop | Hotline HQ
seo.description: Use the hotline from a preconfigured desk phone, your mobile browser, or any desktop. No app to install — push-to-talk from anywhere.
seo.keywords: hotline mobile app, web based hotline, push to talk mobile, desk phone hotline, browser conference call

hero.heading: Desk phone, mobile, or laptop — you're connected
hero.lede: A preconfigured Yealink desk phone for your counter. A web app for the road. Push-to-talk from your pocket. No app store, no install, no hardware required.

problem.heading: Hardware lock-in kills adoption
problem.text: Most hotline systems lock you into proprietary hardware. Desk phone breaks? You're offline. Out on a delivery? Out of the loop. Hotline HQ runs on whatever device you've got — your phone IS the hotline.

steps.title: Desk phone (zero-touch)
steps.desc: Preconfigured Yealink phones ship directly to members. Plug in the ethernet cable and it auto-connects — no setup, no IT department.

steps.title: Mobile web app
steps.desc: Open the browser on any smartphone, log in, and you're on. Push-to-talk bar at the bottom — hold to broadcast, release to mute.

steps.title: Desktop browser
steps.desc: Works on Chrome, Edge, Firefox on any laptop or desktop. Spacebar push-to-talk for hands-free operation. Ctrl+L to toggle mute.

steps.title: Seamless switching
steps.desc: Start the morning on the desk phone, switch to mobile for an afternoon delivery run. The network follows you.

benefits.title: No app store dependency
benefits.desc: The web app is a PWA — installable on the home screen but doesn't need the App Store or Google Play. Works straight from the browser.

benefits.title: Push-to-talk on mobile
benefits.desc: A full-width PTT bar at the bottom of the mobile screen. Hold to talk, release to mute — just like a walkie-talkie.

benefits.title: Keyboard shortcuts on desktop
benefits.desc: Spacebar for push-to-talk, Ctrl+L to toggle mute. Power users never touch the mouse.

benefits.title: Screen wake lock
benefits.desc: The app keeps your screen on while you're connected. No missed broadcasts because your phone went to sleep.

scenario.heading: Real-world: from counter to delivery truck
scenario.text: A yard rep starts the day at the counter with the Yealink desk phone, hearing and responding to broadcasts. At noon, they hop in the delivery truck, open Hotline HQ on their iPhone, and stay connected. A broadcast comes in for a part on the truck — they hold the PTT bar and respond. The deal closes before they reach the delivery address.

faqs.q: Do I need to install an app from the App Store?
faqs.a: No. Hotline HQ is a Progressive Web App that runs in your mobile browser. You can add it to your home screen for a native app feel, but it's not required.

faqs.q: What happens if I'm on both my desk phone and mobile at the same time?
faqs.a: The system manages your connection automatically. The most recently connected device takes priority.

faqs.q: Does the mobile app work on older phones?
faqs.a: The web app works on any smartphone with a modern browser — Chrome, Safari, Firefox, or Edge. No special hardware needed.

faqs.q: Is the audio quality the same on mobile as the desk phone?
faqs.a: Depends on your internet connection. On Wi-Fi, it's right up there with the desk phone. On cellular, quality can dip but it's still clear enough to close a deal.

faqs.q: Can I use the hotline from my car's Bluetooth?
faqs.a: Yes. The web app hooks into Media Session API, so your car's Bluetooth controls can toggle mute and unmute.

seo.title: Broadcast Recording & History — Every Call Saved | Hotline HQ
seo.description: Every broadcast is recorded automatically with full metadata. Browse history, replay conversations, track response times across the network.
seo.keywords: broadcast recording, call recording hotline, voice network history, broadcast analytics, call replay

hero.heading: Every broadcast, saved and searchable
hero.lede: Every time a member unmutes and speaks, the broadcast is recorded — with full metadata, transcription, and response time tracking. Nothing is lost.

problem.heading: Verbal deals need a paper trail
problem.text: On a live voice network, deals happen in seconds. Without recordings, there's no way to verify what was said, who responded, or if the deal was honored. Broadcast recording puts an automatic paper trail on every interaction.

steps.title: Automatic recording
steps.desc: The moment a member unmutes, FreeSWITCH starts recording audio to a WAV file. No buttons to press, no setup.

steps.title: AI transcription
steps.desc: After the broadcast ends, the audio runs through local Whisper AI. For auto parts broadcasts, year/make/model/part get extracted automatically.

steps.title: Metadata captured
steps.desc: Who spoke, when, for how long, how many listeners were online, whether anyone responded, and how fast — all logged.

steps.title: Searchable history
steps.desc: Admins and members can browse broadcast history with filters by room, date, answered/unanswered status. Every recording has an inline audio player.

benefits.title: Response time tracking
benefits.desc: The system measures how fast the first yard responded to each broadcast — down to the millisecond. Good way to keep a pulse on network health.

benefits.title: AI part extraction
benefits.desc: Whisper transcription plus AI parsing pulls out structured data: year, make, model, trim, part name, and specification from spoken broadcasts.

benefits.title: Shareable broadcast links
benefits.desc: Generate a public link to any broadcast. The recipient sees the audio player, speaker info, responders, and transcription — no login required.

benefits.title: Answered vs. unanswered tracking
benefits.desc: Every broadcast is classified as answered or unanswered. That data drives the marketplace (unanswered get auto-listed) and helps operators spot coverage gaps.

scenario.heading: Real-world: resolving a pricing dispute
scenario.text: A buyer says the yard quoted $200 on the hotline for a radiator, but the yard says it was $250. The admin pulls up the broadcast recording, plays it back, and confirms the quote was $200. Dispute resolved in 30 seconds with audio evidence.

faqs.q: Are all broadcasts recorded automatically?
faqs.a: Yes. Recording starts the moment a member unmutes. No opt-in, no button to press — it's always on.

faqs.q: How long are recordings kept?
faqs.a: Recordings stay on the server indefinitely. Admins can manage storage as needed, but there's no automatic deletion.

faqs.q: Can members access their own broadcast recordings?
faqs.a: Members can listen to broadcasts in the broadcast panel on their dashboard. Admins get full access to all recordings with download.

faqs.q: Does the AI transcription work for all languages?
faqs.a: Whisper supports multiple languages, but the auto-parts extraction (year/make/model parsing) is tuned for English broadcasts.

faqs.q: Can I share a broadcast recording externally?
faqs.a: Yes. Admins can generate a shareable public link for any broadcast. The link shows the audio player, speaker info, and transcription on a branded page — no login needed.

seo.title: Real-Time Caller ID — Know Who's Talking | Hotline HQ
seo.description: See the company name, rep name, phone number, and city of every speaker in real time. Caller ID on a voice hotline network built for speed.
seo.keywords: caller ID hotline, real-time caller ID, voice network caller identification, who is talking hotline

hero.heading: Know who's talking before they finish speaking
hero.lede: Every broadcast shows the speaker's company name, rep name, phone number, and city — in real time on every member's screen.

problem.heading: Anonymous calls don't close deals
problem.text: On a traditional conference line, voices are anonymous. You don't know who's asking or where they're calling from. That uncertainty slows responses and kills trust. Caller ID strips out the guesswork — you see exactly who's broadcasting and where they are.

steps.title: Member unmutes
steps.desc: When a member picks up the hotline phone or taps unmute, the system identifies them by SIP registration and MAC address.

steps.title: Caller ID pushed instantly
steps.desc: The server pushes the speaker's profile — company name, rep name, phone number, city — to every connected device via Server-Sent Events.

steps.title: Displayed on every screen
steps.desc: The web dashboard, mobile app, and admin panel all show the active speaker's info in real time with color-coded status.

steps.title: History preserved
steps.desc: Every broadcast is logged with full caller ID. Members and admins can review who spoke, when, and for how long.

benefits.title: Instant trust
benefits.desc: When someone responds to a broadcast, the original speaker can see the responder's company and location. They know they're dealing with a real business.

benefits.title: Faster responses
benefits.desc: Members respond faster when they know who's asking. A yard in Phoenix is more likely to jump on a broadcast from a shop in their metro area.

benefits.title: Accountability
benefits.desc: Every broadcast is attributed. No anonymous noise, no mystery callers. Keeps the network professional and productive.

benefits.title: Admin visibility
benefits.desc: Network operators see every active speaker across all rooms in real time — who's talking, for how long, and whether anyone responded.

scenario.heading: Real-world: responding to a local broadcast
scenario.text: A member hears a request for a 2020 Honda Civic transmission. On their dashboard, they see: "Desert Auto Recyclers / Mike / (602) 555-0123 / Phoenix, AZ." They know it's a local yard 15 miles away, so they unmute and respond. The caller sees "Sunbelt Auto Parts / Rosa / Tempe, AZ" and knows the part can be delivered same-day. Deal done in under 10 seconds.

faqs.q: What information is shown in the caller ID?
faqs.a: Company name, rep name, phone number, and city. Pulled from the member's profile — whatever they entered at signup or in account settings.

faqs.q: Does caller ID work on the desk phone too?
faqs.a: The desk phone shows a compact caller ID on its screen. The full view — company, city, phone number — shows up on the web dashboard and mobile app.

faqs.q: Can members hide their caller ID?
faqs.a: No. Caller ID is always visible to maintain network trust and accountability. That's a feature, not a limitation — it's what makes the network work.

faqs.q: How fast does the caller ID appear?
faqs.a: Pushed via SSE the moment a member unmutes. Typical latency is under 200 milliseconds — it shows up before the speaker finishes their first word.

seo.title: Private Direct Calls — Extension-Based Calling | Hotline HQ
seo.description: Every member gets a 3-digit extension for private calls over the network. No phone bills, no long-distance charges. Call any yard directly.
seo.keywords: private hotline calls, extension based calling, direct call network, free business calls, intercom hotline

hero.heading: Private calls, zero phone bills
hero.lede: Every member gets a 3-digit extension number. Dial any member privately over the network — negotiate pricing, discuss details, close the deal. No phone bills, no long-distance charges.

problem.heading: Follow-up calls shouldn't cost money
problem.text: After a broadcast, members often need a private follow-up — negotiate pricing, confirm fitment, arrange delivery. Calling the member's cell or landline costs money and pulls them off the hotline. Direct calls keep the conversation on-network. Free and fast.

steps.title: Request an extension
steps.desc: Members pick any 3-digit number (100-999) from the member directory in their dashboard. The extension gets assigned right away.

steps.title: Dial *NNN from the hotline
steps.desc: From the desk phone, dial star plus the 3-digit extension (e.g., *247). From the web app, tap the "Call" button next to any member's name.

steps.title: Private connection
steps.desc: Both parties get pulled out of the conference room into a private call. The conversation is recorded and both members return to the room when the call ends.

steps.title: Missed call notifications
steps.desc: If the callee doesn't answer within 15 seconds, they get a push notification with the caller's name and extension — one tap to call back.

benefits.title: Zero cost
benefits.desc: Direct calls travel over the internet, not the phone network. No per-minute charges, no long-distance fees.

benefits.title: Searchable member directory
benefits.desc: A full directory of every member on the network — searchable by company name, city, phone number, or extension.

benefits.title: Call recording
benefits.desc: Every direct call is recorded automatically. Handy for dispute resolution, training, and quality checks.

benefits.title: Push notification on miss
benefits.desc: Miss a direct call? You get a push notification with the caller's details. One tap to call back.

scenario.heading: Real-world: closing a deal privately
scenario.text: A yard hears a broadcast requesting a 2018 F-150 transfer case. They have one, but they want to negotiate price privately. They check the member directory, see the broadcaster is "Valley Auto Recyclers / ext. 315" and dial *315. A private call connects in 2 seconds. They agree on $450 with free delivery, hang up, and both return to the conference room automatically.

faqs.q: How do I get an extension number?
faqs.a: Go to the Members page in your dashboard and tap "Request Extension." Pick any available 3-digit number between 100 and 999.

faqs.q: Can I call someone who doesn't have an extension?
faqs.a: No — both parties need an extension for direct calls. Takes 10 seconds to register one.

faqs.q: Are direct calls recorded?
faqs.a: Yes. All direct calls are recorded and available in the admin dashboard. Protects both parties and helps resolve disputes.

faqs.q: What happens if nobody answers?
faqs.a: After 15 seconds of ringing, the call is declined automatically. The callee gets a push notification with the caller's name and can call back with one tap.

faqs.q: Can I make a direct call from my mobile?
faqs.a: Yes. The member directory on the web app has a "Call" button next to every member with an extension. Tap it and the call connects over the internet.

seo.title: Enterprise Security — Production-Grade Voice Network | Hotline HQ
seo.description: JWT authentication, rate limiting, SIP digest auth, encrypted connections, and role-based access control. Built for always-on, production voice networks.
seo.keywords: secure hotline, enterprise voice security, SIP authentication, encrypted conference, production grade hotline

hero.heading: Built for always-on production
hero.lede: JWT authentication, rate limiting, SIP digest auth, encrypted WebSocket connections, and role-based access control. The infrastructure runs 24/7 with zero planned downtime.

problem.heading: Voice networks are targets
problem.text: An always-on voice network connected to hundreds of SIP phones is a prime target for toll fraud, unauthorized access, and abuse. Security can't be bolted on later — it has to be the foundation.

steps.title: SIP digest authentication
steps.desc: Every phone must authenticate with FreeSWITCH using SIP digest auth before joining a room. Unauthorized User-Agents get silently rejected.

steps.title: JWT token auth
steps.desc: The web client uses JWT access tokens (15-minute expiry) with refresh tokens (7-day expiry). Tokens rotate automatically.

steps.title: Rate limiting & lockout
steps.desc: Login attempts are capped at 5 per 15 minutes per IP. After 5 failures, the account locks for 15 minutes.

steps.title: Encrypted transport
steps.desc: All web traffic runs over HTTPS/TLS. SIP signaling uses WSS (WebSocket Secure). Nothing goes over plaintext.

benefits.title: UA allowlist
benefits.desc: Only Yealink phones and the Hotline HQ web client can register. Everything else gets rejected. Persistent offenders go on the blocklist.

benefits.title: Role-based admin access
benefits.desc: Three admin roles: Admin (full), Editor (users/rooms/notifications), Analytics (read-only). Principle of least privilege.

benefits.title: Listener session isolation
benefits.desc: Public listen-in sessions use ephemeral SIP credentials with 60-second auth TTL, single-use tokens, and a 20 concurrent session cap.

benefits.title: Security headers
benefits.desc: X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy, and Permissions-Policy headers on every response. Server fingerprint is suppressed.

scenario.heading: Real-world: blocking a toll fraud attempt
scenario.text: An unknown SIP User-Agent tries to register with the FreeSWITCH server. The directory endpoint checks the UA string, finds it's not a Yealink or web client, and returns a 403. The admin sees the blocked attempt in the SIP logs and adds the UA to the blocklist. The attacker never reaches the conference.

faqs.q: Is call audio encrypted?
faqs.a: SIP signaling is encrypted via WSS. The audio stream uses SRTP when the endpoint supports it. All web traffic goes over HTTPS.

faqs.q: Can unauthorized phones connect to the network?
faqs.a: No. The FreeSWITCH directory endpoint validates every registration against the account database and checks the SIP User-Agent. Unknown devices get rejected.

faqs.q: How are admin accounts secured?
faqs.a: Bcrypt-hashed passwords, JWT tokens with short expiry, refresh token rotation, and rate-limited login with account lockout after failed attempts.

faqs.q: Is there an audit log?
faqs.a: Yes. Every event worth tracking — joins, leaves, mutes, unmutes, broadcasts, direct calls, room changes, admin actions — gets logged with timestamps.

faqs.q: What happens during a server restart?
faqs.a: The server shuts down gracefully: active calls receive a BYE signal, recordings are saved, and the process exits clean. Phones reconnect on their own when the server comes back.

seo.title: Multi-Language Support — 6 Languages + RTL | Hotline HQ
seo.description: The Hotline HQ member dashboard is available in English, Spanish, French, Chinese, Japanese, and Arabic with full RTL support.
seo.keywords: multi-language hotline, spanish hotline app, RTL support, i18n voice network, multilingual dashboard

hero.heading: Your members' language, not yours
hero.lede: The member dashboard detects your system language and loads the right translation automatically. Six languages with full RTL support — your network shouldn't have a language barrier.

problem.heading: English-only tools lose members
problem.text: A big chunk of the dismantler workforce speaks Spanish first. An English-only interface creates friction, slows adoption, and makes people feel like the tool wasn't built for them.

steps.title: Auto-detection
steps.desc: On first visit, the app detects the browser language and loads the matching translation. Nothing to configure.

steps.title: Manual switch
steps.desc: Members can switch languages anytime from the sidebar selector. Takes effect right away — no page reload.

steps.title: Full coverage
steps.desc: Every UI element is translated: navigation, buttons, forms, error messages, help overlays, notifications, and the push-to-talk bar.

steps.title: RTL layout
steps.desc: Arabic translations include full right-to-left layout — text direction, icon positions, and navigation all flip correctly.

benefits.title: 6 languages
benefits.desc: English, Spanish, French, Chinese (Simplified), Japanese, and Arabic. The most common languages in the global auto parts trade.

benefits.title: Faster onboarding
benefits.desc: Spanish-speaking members can sign up, set up their profile, and start using the hotline entirely in Spanish. Zero English required.

benefits.title: Inclusive network
benefits.desc: Language support shows the network is built for everyone — not just English speakers. That matters when you're growing in diverse markets.

benefits.title: Lazy-loaded bundles
benefits.desc: Translation files load on demand. English users never download Spanish translations. Keeps the initial load fast no matter how many languages we add.

scenario.heading: Real-world: a Spanish-speaking yard joins the network
scenario.text: A dismantler in East LA opens the signup page. The app detects Spanish and loads the interface in Spanish. They complete signup, see the dashboard in their language, and immediately get the push-to-talk bar: "Mantener para Hablar." On the hotline and productive in under 2 minutes — no training, no English required.

faqs.q: What languages are supported?
faqs.a: English, Spanish, French, Chinese (Simplified), Japanese, and Arabic. More can be added as the network expands to new regions.

faqs.q: Does the voice audio get translated?
faqs.a: No — translations cover the dashboard interface only. Voice broadcasts are in whatever language the speaker uses. The hotline is a live voice channel.

faqs.q: Can I force a specific language for all members?
faqs.a: Members pick their own language. There's no way to force one — by design. People work faster in whatever language they're comfortable with.

faqs.q: Is the admin dashboard also translated?
faqs.a: The admin dashboard is English-only for now. The member-facing app (signup, login, dashboard, settings) is fully translated in all 6 languages.

seo.title: Smart Notifications — Never Miss a Part Request | Hotline HQ
seo.description: Push notifications on mobile, Telegram alerts, and lock screen controls keep your members connected even when they're away from the hotline.
seo.keywords: hotline notifications, push notifications, telegram alerts, missed call notifications, parts request alerts

hero.heading: Your members never miss a request
hero.lede: Push notifications on mobile, Telegram alerts for room activity, and lock screen mute controls. Even when a member steps away, the network reaches them.

problem.heading: Members can't be at the phone 24/7
problem.text: Even on an always-on network, members step away — lunch, deliveries, customers at the counter. Without notifications, missed broadcasts turn into lost revenue. Notifications close that gap between the hotline and the member's pocket.

steps.title: Web push notifications
steps.desc: When a broadcast goes unanswered in a member's room, they get a push notification on their phone — even if the browser is closed. Tap to open the dashboard.

steps.title: Telegram integration
steps.desc: Broadcasts get forwarded to Telegram channels with full details: speaker name, room, duration, transcription, and part details. Configurable per room.

steps.title: Direct call alerts
steps.desc: When someone dials a member's extension and they don't pick up, a push notification shows the caller's name. One tap to call back.

steps.title: Lock screen controls
steps.desc: On mobile, media controls in the notification shade let members mute and unmute without opening the app.

benefits.title: Works even when closed
benefits.desc: Web push notifications come from the browser's service worker — the Hotline HQ tab doesn't need to be open.

benefits.title: Configurable per room
benefits.desc: Operators choose which rooms trigger notifications, filter by answered/unanswered, and skip broadcasts with no detected parts.

benefits.title: Critical member alerts
benefits.desc: Flag important members as "critical" — if they go offline, the system sends Telegram alerts every 60 seconds until they reconnect.

benefits.title: iOS support via PWA
benefits.desc: On iPhone, members add Hotline HQ to their home screen to get push notifications. The app walks them through it.

scenario.heading: Real-world: catching a request during lunch
scenario.text: A yard rep steps out for lunch. While they're eating, someone broadcasts for a part they have stacked in the back. They get a push notification: "CA Room — Looking for 2017 Accord hood." They tap the notification, open the dashboard, hold the PTT bar, and respond: "Got it, black, $120." The deal closes from the restaurant parking lot.

faqs.q: Do I need to install an app for push notifications?
faqs.a: On Android, notifications work in Chrome with no install. On iPhone, you need to add Hotline HQ to your home screen first (Share > Add to Home Screen).

faqs.q: Can I turn off notifications?
faqs.a: Yes. Members control their notification preferences in Account Settings — parts request alerts and direct call alerts can be toggled independently.

faqs.q: What notification channels are available?
faqs.a: Web push (mobile and desktop), Telegram (text or voice message), and WhatsApp. Each channel is configured separately by the network operator.

faqs.q: Do notifications include the audio recording?
faqs.a: Telegram notifications can include the broadcast audio as a voice message. Push notifications link to the broadcast in the dashboard where you can play it back.

seo.title: Parts Marketplace — SEO-Powered Part Listings | Hotline HQ
seo.description: Every captured broadcast becomes a searchable part listing on Google. The marketplace turns hotline activity into organic traffic and new member signups.
seo.keywords: used auto parts marketplace, parts wanted board, auto parts listings, used parts search, salvage parts marketplace

hero.heading: Your hotline activity, indexed by Google
hero.lede: Every unanswered broadcast automatically becomes a searchable part listing. SEO-optimized pages with structured data bring organic traffic — and new members — from Google.

problem.heading: Hotline networks are invisible to search engines
problem.text: A traditional hotline generates zero online presence. Hundreds of part requests fly through daily, but Google never sees any of them. The marketplace changes that — voice activity becomes web pages that rank, pull in visitors, and convert them into network members.

steps.title: Broadcast captured
steps.desc: Unanswered broadcasts are transcribed and parsed for part details. Each one becomes a marketplace listing with year, make, model, part, room, and timestamp.

steps.title: SEO page generated
steps.desc: Each listing gets its own URL (e.g., /parts/2019-toyota-camry-bumper-ca-12345) with a unique title, meta description, and JSON-LD structured data.

steps.title: Dynamic sitemap
steps.desc: A separate marketplace sitemap is auto-generated and submitted to Google so new listings get crawled and indexed fast.

steps.title: Response form
steps.desc: Visitors can respond to any listing by submitting their name and phone number. The broadcaster gets an email with the respondent's details.

benefits.title: Organic traffic engine
benefits.desc: Every listing is a new page that can rank on Google. Over time, the marketplace builds into a real source of organic traffic.

benefits.title: Member acquisition
benefits.desc: Visitors who land on a listing see how active the network is and get a prompt to sign up. The marketplace is the top of the funnel.

benefits.title: Structured data
benefits.desc: Every listing includes Product and WantAction JSON-LD schema, breadcrumbs, and Open Graph tags — built to get maximum search engine visibility.

benefits.title: Related listings
benefits.desc: Each listing page shows related parts from the same room. Keeps visitors browsing longer and increases time on site.

scenario.heading: Real-world: a Google search becomes a new member
scenario.text: A shop in Houston Googles "used 2020 Honda CR-V catalytic converter Texas." They find a Hotline HQ marketplace listing from yesterday's unanswered broadcast. They fill out the response form. The yard calls back and sells the part. The shop asks, "How do I hear these requests live?" They sign up that afternoon.

faqs.q: Do I need to manually create marketplace listings?
faqs.a: No. Listings are created automatically from unanswered broadcast transcriptions. The whole pipeline is hands-off.

faqs.q: How quickly do listings appear on Google?
faqs.a: New listings hit the dynamic sitemap right away. Google typically indexes them within 24-48 hours, depending on crawl frequency.

faqs.q: Can I see how much traffic the marketplace generates?
faqs.a: Yes. The marketplace pages include Microsoft Clarity tracking. Traffic data shows up in your Clarity dashboard.

faqs.q: Are listings visible to non-members?
faqs.a: Yes. The marketplace is fully public — that's the point. Anyone can find a listing on Google and respond.

seo.title: Unanswered Broadcast Capture — No Request Wasted | Hotline HQ
seo.description: When a broadcast goes unanswered, it's automatically transcribed, parsed for year/make/model, and listed on the parts marketplace. Every request becomes a lead.
seo.keywords: unanswered calls, missed call capture, auto parts leads, broadcast transcription, lead generation hotline

hero.heading: No request goes to waste
hero.lede: When a broadcast goes unanswered, the system doesn't just log it — it transcribes the audio, extracts the part details, and creates a searchable marketplace listing that lives on Google 24/7.

problem.heading: Unanswered calls are lost revenue
problem.text: Some broadcasts go unanswered — the right yard was at lunch, or the part is rare. On a traditional hotline, that request vanishes. On Hotline HQ, every unanswered broadcast becomes a searchable listing that any yard can respond to later — or that a Google searcher finds on their own.

steps.title: Broadcast goes unanswered
steps.desc: The system waits 5 seconds after the speaker mutes. If nobody unmutes, the broadcast gets tagged as unanswered.

steps.title: AI transcription runs
steps.desc: Whisper AI transcribes the audio locally. Then an AI model parses out the structured data: year, make, model, trim, part, and specification.

steps.title: Marketplace listing created
steps.desc: The extracted part details become a listing on the Parts Marketplace — with the speaker's room, time, and a "Respond" form.

steps.title: Indexed by Google
steps.desc: Each listing gets its own SEO-optimized page with Product and WantAction schema. Google indexes these pages, driving organic traffic to the network.

benefits.title: Passive lead generation
benefits.desc: Unanswered broadcasts become Google-indexed pages. Someone searching "2019 Camry bumper California" could land on your marketplace listing.

benefits.title: Delayed responses
benefits.desc: A yard that was closed when the broadcast aired can find the listing the next morning and respond. The broadcaster gets an email notification.

benefits.title: Network growth driver
benefits.desc: Marketplace listings attract visitors who aren't on the network yet. They see the activity, see the speed, and sign up.

benefits.title: Zero manual work
benefits.desc: The whole pipeline — recording, transcription, parsing, listing creation, SEO — is fully automated. Operators don't lift a finger.

scenario.heading: Real-world: a Google search closes a deal 12 hours later
scenario.text: At 8 PM, a yard broadcasts for a rare Mercedes part. The rooms are quiet — unanswered. The system transcribes it, creates a listing: "2016 Mercedes C300 turbocharger — California." The next morning, a Google searcher finds the page, fills out the response form with their name and phone number. The broadcaster gets an email, calls back, and sells the part.

faqs.q: How does the system know a broadcast is unanswered?
faqs.a: After the speaker mutes, the system waits 5 seconds for another member to unmute. No response in that window means unanswered.

faqs.q: Can answered broadcasts also be listed on the marketplace?
faqs.a: Right now, only unanswered broadcasts get auto-listed. Answered ones are assumed resolved on the hotline.

faqs.q: How accurate is the AI part extraction?
faqs.a: The system uses Whisper for transcription and a specialized AI model for part extraction. It picks up year, make, model, and common part names reliably. Heavy accents or unusual parts might need a manual look.

faqs.q: Can I respond to a marketplace listing if I'm not on the network?
faqs.a: Yes. Anyone can view the listing and submit a response with their name and phone number. The broadcaster gets an email with the details.

faqs.q: Do marketplace listings expire?
faqs.a: Listings stay visible but get marked as stale after a while. Fresh ones (under 2 hours) show a "fresh" tag.
